## TL;DR ‚Äî Content Taxonomy session

**Goal:** make proactive-context's existing capture artifacts (guides, claims, episode cards, research records, nouns, transcripts) first-class, type-aware system plumbing ‚Äî without turning it into a generic wiki. Executed the 8-phase plan in `Plans/content-taxonomy-implementation-experiment-plan.md`.

**Setup:** isolated git worktree `../proactive-context-taxonomy` on branch `taxonomy-work`; fanned out parallel sub-agents over disjoint files.

### What we built (7 commits, 300 tests green, every new flag default-OFF / byte-identical to baseline)
- **Phase 0** ‚Äî `pc debug taxonomy` audit command + frozen baseline doc.
- **Phase 1** ‚Äî `src/content_kind.rs`: central `ContentKind`/`Currentness`/`Authority`/`ClaimOp` type model.
- **Phase 2** ‚Äî typed selector catalog + `research:`/`noun:` source rows (`PC_TYPED_CATALOG` / `PC_RESEARCH_CATALOG` / `PC_NOUN_CATALOG`).
- **Phase 3** ‚Äî source-type SELECT guidance (`PC_SELECT_SOURCE_TYPES`) ‚Äî *code only*.
- **Phase 4** ‚Äî `ClaimStatus{Settled,Proposed,Unknown}` persisted on claims; `PC_CLAIM_STATUS` keeps proposed ideas out of current-guide prose.
- **Phase 6** ‚Äî canonical `TranscriptTurn` model + lossless projection.
- **Phase 7** ‚Äî `pc wiki backfill-taxonomy`: idempotent, non-destructive typed index (verified byte-identical on rerun).
- **Deferred (your call):** Phase 5 claim catalog (plan says wait for reviewed cluster summaries); Phase 3 A0‚ÄìA5 eval arms.

### Results / experiments observed
**Inventory audit** (point-in-time): heavy historical/evidence corpus ‚Äî **100 research records + 159 episode cards (66 active)** ‚Äî was largely **invisible to the selector**, and guides were over-fragmented (**57 guides across 28 topics, ~12 singletons**). This is the core problem the typed catalog targets.

**Baseline probe eval** (r3: 30 history / 52 future sessions, 40 labels, 10 reversals; judge `glm-5.1:cloud`). Reference numbers for the wiki/current-guide path:

| Metric | Baseline |
|---|---|
| Guide restatement recall | 75% |
| User-direction recall | 71.4% |
| Stale-current leak ("the sin") | 2/10 |
| Trajectory X‚ÜíY recoverable | 9/10 |
| Latency p50 / p95 | 5.0s / 12.7s |

Frozen labels are reusable via `--score-only`, so the deferred A0‚ÄìA5 arms will be cheap to run when you want to validate flipping a default.

### Notable incidents
- **Eval harness bug found:** `pc eval` auto-overwrote `claims-first-validation-results.md`, destroying **~1475 lines of accumulated Runs 11‚Äì12 history** ‚Üí I reverted it and flagged the team. Real bug: the harness should append/version, not clobber.
- **Multi-agent hazard confirmed:** `docs/wiki/` is untracked/generated; a concurrent peer session regenerated it mid-run (57‚Üí3 guides). My work was unaffected (isolated branch, all in `src/`).

**Net:** the full plan is implemented as inert, flag-gated infrastructure with a measured baseline to validate against. Remaining work is validation/rollout decisions, not build-out.

Results posted to team chat (`6046d85a`). Here's the close-out.

## Phase 3 arms ‚Äî experimental result

Ran the **real** inject path (catalog + SELECT + COMPILE) over the frozen baseline labels for each flag combo. Directional (n=20/10, single judge):

| Arm | Flag added | Recall | Stale-leak | Trajectory | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| A0 | ‚Äî | 60% | 1/10 | 7/10 | baseline |
| **A1** | `PC_TYPED_CATALOG` | **70%** | **0/10** | **9/10** | **clean win** (also faster) |
| A2 | +`PC_SELECT_SOURCE_TYPES` | 70% | 0/10 | 7/10 | more decisive, but under-picks episodes |
| A3 | +`PC_RESEARCH_CATALOG` | 55% | 1/10 | 6/10 | hurts on thin corpus ‚Äî keep off |
| A4 | +`PC_NOUN_CATALOG` | vacuous | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | no nouns in store |

**Most interesting finding:** A2's source-type guidance cut episode-card selections 24‚Üí9 and raised hard "contained" hits 4‚Üí7 (the intended precision effect) ‚Äî but that *same* choosiness dropped trajectory recall, because episode cards are the trajectory-recovery source. So the source-type prompt is over-suppressing exactly the artifact class that answers "why did this change." That's a concrete, tunable lesson, not just a number.

## Net state of the whole effort

**Plan fully executed** ‚Äî Phases 0,1,2,3,4,6,7 landed across **11 commits** on `taxonomy-work`, 302 tests green, every flag default-OFF and inert. We now have:
- A measured **baseline** (75% guide recall, 2/10 stale-leak, etc.).
- A **working arm harness** to validate any flag before flipping it.
- **Evidence** that A1 (typed catalog) is a safe quality win and a clear path for the rest.

2. **Tune A2's** SELECT prompt to stop under-picking episodes, then re-run.
3. **Phase 5** (claim catalog) ‚Äî still deferred pending reviewed cluster summaries.

## A2 tuning: result

**The fix worked** (mechanistically, on the signal that *isn't* judge-dependent):
- Episode-card selections recovered **9 ‚Üí 25** ‚Äî the gate no longer avoids historical cards.
- Trajectory rose to **8/10** (‚â• baseline), stale-leak stayed **0** ‚Äî regression resolved at no cost.

**But the bigger finding** is a methodology correction I have to be honest about: re-running the *identical* arms on the *identical* labels made **recall flip sign** (A1's delta vs A0 went **+10 ‚Üí ‚àí25**; A0 itself moved 60‚Üí75%). At n=20 with a single non-deterministic judge, recall deltas ‚â§ ~20pt are **noise**. So my earlier "A1 is a clean win on recall" call **doesn't replicate ‚Äî retracted.**

What survives both runs as trustworthy: the A2‚Ä≤ episode fix, **stale-leak ‚âà 0 under every arm** (no current-truth regression ‚Äî the key safety gate), and A3/research never helping on the thin 4-record corpus.

## Where this leaves Phase 3

- **A2‚Ä≤ is the right prompt** and is committed; all flags stay **default-OFF** (correctly ‚Äî nothing earned a flip).
- The real gate to sign off Phase 3 / flip any default is now a **methodology upgrade**, not more prompt tuning: **n‚â•40 labels AND 3+ judge passes averaged** (or a stronger/deterministic judge) so recall is actually resolvable. Everything's recorded in `taxonomy-phase3-arms-2026-06-17.md` (Run 2 section).

This is a good stopping point: the full plan is built (Phases 0‚Äì4, 6, 7), inert and tested behind flags; we have a baseline, a working arm harness, a tuned A2‚Ä≤, and ‚Äî importantly ‚Äî a clear-eyed read that the current eval can't yet justify turning anything on.
